login.title: Sign In
login.subtitle: Backup management system
login.description: Enter your credentials to access the dashboard.

users.title: User Management
users.subtitle: Manage who can access the backup system

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Backup system overview
dashboard.no_runs: No backup runs yet. Create a job to get started.

sources.title: Backup Sources
sources.subtitle: Manage your backup data sources
sources.delete_msg: Are you sure? All associated jobs will also be deleted.

form.name: Source Name
form.path_hint: Absolute path inside container or on server
form.docker_hint_body: If this app runs in Docker, mount your host directory as a volume:
form.docker_hint_then: Then use the container path, e.g.

destinations.title: Destinations
destinations.subtitle: Manage where backups are stored
destinations.delete_msg: This will delete the destination and all associated jobs.

form.name: Destination Name
form.endpoint_hint: For S3-compatible storage (MinIO, Backblaze, Wasabi, etc.)
form.path_prefix_hint: Optional folder prefix in the bucket
form.force_path_style_label: Use path-style URLs (required for some S3-compatible services)
form.passphrase_hint: If your private key is encrypted
form.local_path_hint: Absolute path inside the container or on the server
form.password_sftp_hint: Leave empty if using private key
form.docker_hint_body: If this app runs in Docker, mount the host directory as a volume:

jobs.title: Backup Jobs
jobs.subtitle: Scheduled and on-demand backup jobs
jobs.delete_msg: This will permanently delete the job and all its run history.

form.name: Job Name
form.destinations_hint: The backup will be sent to all selected destinations
form.schedule_hint: Cron expression (minute hour day month weekday)
form.status_enabled: Enabled - job will run on schedule

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.subtitle: HTTP notifications for backup events (Discord, Slack, WhatsApp and more)
webhooks.delete_msg: Are you sure you want to delete this webhook?

form.name: Name
form.url_discord_hint: Copy from: Server Settings → Integrations → Webhooks
form.url_slack_hint: Copy from: Slack App → Incoming Webhooks
form.url_generic_hint: Any HTTP/HTTPS endpoint that accepts a JSON POST
form.secret_generic_hint: Optional HMAC-SHA256 key to verify request authenticity
form.secret_openwa_hint: Bearer token for OpenWA API authentication

ssh.title: SSH Servers
ssh.subtitle: SSH connections to remote servers and clusters
ssh.delete_msg: Are you sure? Associated backup sources will lose their SSH connection.
ssh.key_installed: SSH key was automatically installed on the server
ssh.key_installing_bg: Key installation is running in background — check Server Logs to confirm success
ssh.key_manual: Copy the public key and manually add it to authorized_keys on the server
ssh.public_key_label: SSH Public Key (add to authorized_keys on the server)

form.name: Name
form.password_hint_new: Provide password to automatically install the key on the server. The password is not stored.
form.password_hint_edit: Provide password to install the key on the server if not already installed.

admin.title: Create Admin Account
admin.subtitle: Set up the administrator account you'll use to log in.

welcome.title: Welcome to Infinix Backup
welcome.subtitle: Automated backup system for databases, Docker containers and files. Let's configure it in a few steps.

database.title: Database Connection
database.subtitle: Connect Infinix Backup to your PostgreSQL database.

security.title: Security Keys
security.hint: Auto-generated random keys. Store them somewhere safe — if lost, encrypted data cannot be recovered.

restart.title: Restarting Application
restart.subtitle: Configuration saved. The application is restarting to apply the database settings.
restart.warning: Do not close or refresh this page

install.title: Setting Up Database
install.subtitle: Running database migrations, this may take a few seconds...

complete.title: Setup Complete!
complete.subtitle: Infinix Backup is ready. You can now add backup sources, destinations and create scheduled jobs.
complete.tip_1: Add a Backup Source (PostgreSQL, MySQL, Docker container or files)
complete.tip_2: Add a Destination (S3, FTP, SFTP or local filesystem)
complete.tip_3: Create a Backup Job and set a schedule
